title: AI Deployment Strategist
requirements_met: Design and deploy custom AI agents for customer workflows: Ryan builds AI agents end-to-end with Claude Code
Partner with stakeholders / bridge customers and product/engineering: ran cross-functional GTM and community feedback loops at Minima
Programming (Python/SQL preferred): Ryan is self-taught in Python, TypeScript, Rust, SQL
Familiarity with SaaS platforms and data concepts: data analytics (Google Analytics, Amazon Alexa) + AI tooling
gaps: Listing requires an engineering or computer-science degree from a top-tier institution plus 2-5 years in technical/implementation roles with strong data-modeling depth - Ryan is self-taught (BA Management, Leeds) and his technical depth is recent/AI-agent-focused rather than formal data-modeling/implementation experience
preferred_met: Background in FP&A or financial modeling: partial - fundraising support exposure but not FP&A

required_skills.calculation: 40 * (4/5) = 32 (missing formal CS/engineering degree + 2-5yr technical implementation depth)

preferred_skills.calculation: 20 * (~0.1/3) - minimal nice-to-have coverage (no FP&A, no Pigment experience, no extra languages) = 2

experience_fit.calculation: Years align with 2-5yr band but the role is technical-implementation/strategist with formal modeling depth Ryan lacks = 10/15

industry_match.calculation: Pigment is an AI-powered business planning platform deploying custom AI agents - genuine AI company = 15

location_match.calculation: London; user open to London hybrid/in-person = 10

notes: Pigment: AI-powered business planning platform. AI Deployment Strategist designs/deploys custom AI agents for customers, translating business problems into Pigment models. AI-agent work is a match for Ryan's interests, but the role's formal CS/engineering-degree + technical-implementation-depth requirements are the main gap. Biggest fit gap: top-tier CS/engineering degree + deep data-modeling/implementation track record.

must_haves.req: Engineering or computer science degree from top-tier institution
must_haves.note: Ryan has a non-technical BA Business Management (2:1, Leeds). Hard-listed under 'Required'.

must_haves.req: 2-5 yrs in a technical/client-facing/implementation role (FDE, Data Scientist/AI Engineer, Solutions Architect/Engineer, Implementation Consultant)
must_haves.note: Ryan has no roles of this type — his background is GTM/community/founder's-associate, not technical implementation/solutions engineering.

must_haves.req: Data modeling, analytics, or business-logic design + AI/ML concepts
must_haves.note: Some analytics (Amazon Alexa query analysis, GA) and AI tooling, but no professional data-modeling/business-logic-design depth.
Operator-level coding via Claude Code; no formal Python/SQL production proficiency.

must_haves.req: Manage multiple stakeholders/projects in fast-paced environments
must_haves.note: Strong multi-workstream startup operator track record.

hard_blockers: Top-tier CS/Engineering degree explicitly REQUIRED — Ryan does not have one.
2-5 yrs in a named technical-implementation role (FDE/Data Scientist/Solutions Architect/Implementation Consultant) explicitly REQUIRED — Ryan has none of these.
Domain is enterprise FP&A / business-planning modeling (Unilever/Siemens), not Ryan's crypto/GTM domain.
met_strengths: Genuine interest and hands-on agentic-AI building
interview_match.one_line_verdict: Two explicit hard-required gates (top-tier CS/Eng degree + 2-5 yrs in a technical-implementation role) that Ryan does not meet; despite genuine agent interest this is the weakest fit of the five.
